name: John Patitucci
abstract: John Patitucci (born December 22, 1959) is an American jazz bassist and composer.

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: The guys were 15-20 years younger than me... John Patitucci and Dave Weckl—within the group formed a trio... That’s how the Elecktric Band got started. But John came along and just covered the whole...
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: This became a co-led project... Patitucci overdubs the bass guitar. I really feel really great that we actually documented how we felt and how we approached the music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: And I did another date with Jack DeJohnette and John Patitucci. But, these two albums were the objects of the promotion.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terri Lyne Carrington
about_quotes.evidence: I have talked to Herbie, John Patitucci and Wallace Roney and Mike Stern and Kirk Whalum and Wayne Shorter, which I thought was a good start to really try to look further into this.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terri Lyne Carrington interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Drummond
about_quotes.evidence: And then I’ll listen to Rufus Reid, Ron Carter, Buster Williams and Cecil McBee, George Mraz, you know John Patitucci, all these different guys will actually...
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Drummond interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Shorter
about_quotes.evidence: When John and I talked, he said, ‘My wife...” Sachi, her name is.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Shorter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
